free_text: Triton and alpha-particle production in neutron-induced
reactions on carbon at En=42.5,62.7 and 72.8 MeV
Prelim.results in graphs at 62.7MeV
The experimental set-up described in details.
Experimental details
Analysis details
Proton  and
deuteron production on carbon at the same energies
- final data
The accelerated proton beam is focused on a
natural lithium target. A quasimonoenergetic neutron
beam was produced at 0 degr. lab. angle. Using a
3-mm-thick lithium target and a 10**(-5)A proton beam,
about 10**6 n/s are available at the location of
reaction chamber (around 3m downstream from the
lithium target).
Four charged-particle detector
telescopes are used simultaneously. Each of them
consists of a delta-E detector (NE102 pl.scin., 0.1
mm thick, 4cm in diam.) and an E detector (CsI(Tl)
crystal, 22 mm thick 38.2mm in diam.). For energy
calibration of the detectors the protons and deuterons
recoiling from a polypropylene (1 or 0.5 mm thick)
and a 0.6-mm-thick deuterated polypropylene target
respectively, are used.
The neutron-beam radial distribution measured
with NE102 scin. ( 5mm in diam.)
An elemental carbon target (5x5 cm2 surface and 1 mm
thick). The angle of the target with the beam was
chosen to minimize the thickness of the target material
traversed the produced charged particles towards
telescopes.
Faraday cup serves as beam monitor.
By S.Benck (2015-05-05): The 42.5 MeV data was not
retained in the final analysis as it did not permit to
profit from its low energy tail in the incident
neutron energy spectrum.
The measured dataset that has been published in
I.Slypen et al.,At.Data Nucl.Data Tables 76(2000)26,
(see 22718 Entry) can effectively be considered as the
final version of carbon related data. The threshold
energies has been uniformly defined during the release
of this data set for all measured spectra at the
various angles.
Total error includes:
The overall relative errors of the points
in the energy spectra are about 7%, given by the
accumulated statistics, except for lower energies,
where they can be larger as a result of the procedure
adopted for the thick target correction
The uncertainty in the cross-section
absolute scale is less than 10% includes following:
The uncertainty in reference (n,p) cr. sect.
The uncertainty in  beam monitoring
The uncertainty in statistics in the H(n,p)
recoil proton peak
The uncertainty in solid-angle corrections
* EN-RSL - half width of the incident neuron energy bin
SD
Correlation established

added. Tabulated data were adopted from 22303 Entry

references.) ERR-ANALYS update.
